articles.title: Adobe embraces conversational AI editing, marking a ‘fundamental shift’ in creative work
articles.summary: Adobe launches Firefly AI Assistant enabling creators to edit content through conversational prompts rather than manual tools, marking a fundamental shift in creative workflows by lowering skill barriers.

articles.title: Grok’s sexual deepfakes almost got it banned from Apple’s App Store. Almost.
articles.summary: Apple threatened to remove Grok from its App Store in January due to nonconsensual sexual deepfakes on X, but took no public action despite widespread criticism of the platform's inability to curb the issue.

articles.title: The attacks on Sam Altman are a warning for the AI world
articles.summary: A 20-year-old allegedly threw a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home citing AI extinction fears. Altman's residence was targeted twice within days, reflecting growing tensions in the AI industry.

articles.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
articles.summary: Allbirds sold its shoe business and pivoted to AI, rebranding as NewBird AI with $50M in convertible financing to focus on AI servers.

articles.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
articles.summary: Reid Hoffman argues that tracking AI token usage helps measure adoption but warns against using it as a standalone productivity metric without broader context.

articles.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
articles.summary: Adobe launches Firefly AI assistant that integrates across Creative Cloud apps including Photoshop, Premiere, and Lightroom to automate creative tasks.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS with a platform that has attracted 2 million developers without marketing spend.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality as a free, open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted talented engineers to the AI customer interview startup.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers due to dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. The competitor says it now has about 30,000 customers, many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment plants, and refineries. Six US agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10
articles.summary: NASA plans to launch a nuclear reactor-powered interplanetary spacecraft to Mars by end of 2028, marking a new era in spaceflight and potentially giving the US an advantage in the space race against China.

articles.title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are using illicit tools sold on Telegram to bypass banks' facial recognition security measures, enabling fraudulent account access and money laundering operations.

articles.title: No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
articles.summary: Scientists are developing synthetic mirror bacteria with reversed molecular structures as an NSF-funded project. The potential benefits and risks of creating these organisms remain uncertain.

articles.title: Meet HoloTab by HCompany. Your AI browser companion.
articles.summary: HoloTab by HCompany is an AI browser companion designed to assist users while browsing. It offers intelligent support and enhanced browsing capabilities.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard GPUs. The technology makes advanced virtual environment generation accessible without requiring expensive specialized hardware.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling AI systems to process and compare text, images, and other data types together for improved semantic understanding.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field, exploring what innovations and challenges lie ahead for artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source software, enabling broader access to agricultural technology innovations and AI-driven farming solutions.

articles.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's Skills feature enables users to discover, save, and remix AI workflows for instant reuse. Transform frequently-used AI prompts into convenient one-click tools.

articles.title: Bringing people together at AI for the Economy Forum
articles.summary: Google is hosting an AI for the Economy Forum in Washington D.C. to bring people together and discuss artificial intelligence's economic impact.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost and latency, offering developers flexible options for their needs.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 enhances spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding to improve autonomous robots' ability to perform real-world tasks through better embodied reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open models yet, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enhances voice interactions with improved precision and reduced latency, delivering more fluid and natural audio AI experiences.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and ChatGPT's interactive visualizations.

articles.title: Citizen developers now have their own Wingman
articles.summary: Emergent released Wingman, an autonomous agent enabling non-technical users to deploy AI agents for managing daily tasks. The platform democratizes software creation for citizen developers across 190 countries.

articles.title: Drones get smarter for large farm holdings
articles.summary: DroneDash Technologies and GEODNET formed GEODASH Aerosystems to develop an advanced agricultural spraying drone that autonomously perceives surroundings and adjusts flight plans without pre-mapping, eliminating manual field surveys.

articles.title: The US-China AI gap closed. The responsible AI gap didn’t
articles.summary: Stanford's 2026 AI Index Report reveals the US-China AI performance gap has closed, but the gap in responsible AI evaluation has widened, with models advancing faster than safety assessments.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster, concise responses impairs their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between speed and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that the harness, not the model itself, creates lasting moats.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Bringing Enhanced Embodied Reasoning and Instrument Reading to Physical AI
articles.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, an embodied reasoning model serving as robots' cognitive brain for real-world tasks. It handles visual understanding, spatial reasoning, and task planning while coordinating with vision-language-action models.

articles.title: Google Launches ‘Skills’ in Chrome: Turning Reusable AI Prompts into One-Click Browser Workflows
articles.summary: Google launched Skills in Chrome, allowing users to save frequently used AI prompts as reusable one-click workflows within Gemini. Rolling out April 14, 2026, this feature streamlines repetitive tasks across Mac, Windows, and ChromeOS.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation of Crawl4AI for Web Crawling, Markdown Generation, JavaScript Execution, and LLM-Based Structured Extraction
articles.summary: Tutorial on Crawl4AI covering practical web crawling with markdown generation, JavaScript execution, and LLM-based structured data extraction for web automation and data collection tasks.

articles.title: I tried a new Linux image editor that's like GIMP and Photoshop - but I'm not sold (yet)
articles.summary: A new Linux image editor aims to compete with GIMP and Photoshop, but initial testing suggests it may not yet fully meet user expectations as a viable alternative.

articles.title: You can use Linux 7.0 on these 7 distros today - here's what to expect
articles.summary: Linux 7.0 introduces full Rust support and an improved scheduler across seven distributions. These enhancements aim to boost performance for both work and gaming applications.

articles.title: 'Like handing out the blueprint to a bank vault': Why AI led one company to abandon open source
articles.summary: A company abandoned its open-source program due to AI security threats, moving to proprietary code to prevent hacking risks from AI systems exploiting publicly available source code.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems when handling long context windows, improving efficiency and performance in AI applications.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: Learn how to implement tool calling with Gemma 4 using Python. This guide covers the open-weights model ecosystem and practical techniques for integrating tool calling functionality.

articles.title: Structured Outputs vs. Function Calling: Which Should Your Agent Use?
articles.summary: The article compares structured outputs and function calling for AI agents, examining how language models handle text-based interactions and output generation.

articles.title: Prefill Is Compute-Bound. Decode Is Memory-Bound. Why Your GPU Shouldn’t Do Both.
articles.summary: Disaggregated LLM inference separates prefill (compute-bound) and decode (memory-bound) operations across different GPUs, enabling 2-4x cost reduction that most ML teams haven't yet adopted.

articles.title: 5 Practical Tips for Transforming Your Batch Data Pipeline into Real-Time: Upcoming Webinar
articles.summary: Learn five practical tips for converting batch data pipelines to real-time processing. An upcoming webinar offers guidance on modernizing your data infrastructure.

articles.title: From Pixels to DNA: Why the Future of Compression Is About Every Kind of Data
articles.summary: Data compression is expanding beyond audio and video to encompass all data types, including DNA. Advanced compression techniques are becoming essential across diverse domains.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories where inference is the primary workload. Enterprises should prioritize cost per token metrics over traditional specifications like FLOPS per dollar when evaluating AI infrastructure TCO.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe is launching a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology to enhance video editing workflows for professional creators.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical world across industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate deployment from virtual training to real-world applications.

articles.title: Create rich, custom tooltips in Amazon Quick Sight
articles.summary: Amazon QuickSight now supports custom sheet tooltips that allow dashboard authors to design rich, interactive tooltips using free-form layouts combining charts, KPIs, and visuals that display on hover.

articles.title: Accelerating decode-heavy LLM inference with speculative decoding on AWS Trainium and vLLM
articles.summary: AWS Trainium with speculative decoding accelerates LLM token generation up to 3x for decode-heavy workloads by using a draft model to propose multiple tokens simultaneously, reducing inference costs.

articles.title: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde: Monitoring AI agents in the revenue cycle with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Rede Mater Dei de Saúde implements 12 AI agents using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to monitor and govern revenue cycle operations across their hospital network, enhancing decision-making and operational sustainability.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: NVIDIA's ALCHEMI toolkit enables custom atomistic simulation workflows for chemistry and materials science, balancing computational accuracy with speed for molecular modeling applications.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA NVbandwidth is an essential tool for measuring GPU memory and interconnect performance in CUDA applications. It helps developers optimize data transfer performance in single and multi-GPU systems.

articles.title: NVIDIA Ising Introduces AI-Powered Workflows to Build Fault-Tolerant Quantum Systems
articles.summary: NVIDIA Ising introduces the first open AI models for quantum processor development, featuring Calibration and Decoding models to address quantum computing's noise challenges and build fault-tolerant systems.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward social media advertisements across various platforms, in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative XR technology. Their unconventional path provided unique perspectives that led to cutting-edge developments in extended reality.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work enhances understanding of LLM decision-making through feature attribution analysis for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that quantify how well noisy measurements distinguish objects using only measurement data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
